name: SkyPilot
tagline: Run AI jobs on any cloud or Kubernetes and land on the cheapest available GPUs
seoTitle: SkyPilot - Run AI Jobs Across Clouds & Kubernetes
metaDescription: SkyPilot is an open-source framework that runs and scales AI workloads on Kubernetes, Slurm, and 20+ clouds, with automatic GPU provisioning and failover.
overview: SkyPilot is a system for running, managing, and scaling AI workloads across any compute you have access to. You describe a job once in a small YAML file, and SkyPilot provisions the resources, syncs your code, runs the job, and cleans up idle machines when it finishes.

It is built for AI teams who want a single way to launch jobs without hand-managing each cloud, and for infra teams who want one control plane over Kubernetes, Slurm, reserved GPUs, and more than 20 cloud providers. Your existing GPU, TPU, and CPU jobs run with no code changes.

Within compute orchestration, SkyPilot sits above individual schedulers and cloud APIs. It handles GPU/TPU/CPU provisioning with smart failover across providers, queues and auto-recovers jobs, and runs everything inside your own cloud accounts and clusters (BYOC).
features: One interface to run jobs on Kubernetes, Slurm, reserved GPUs, or 20+ clouds including AWS, GCP, Azure, OCI, Lambda, RunPod, and Nebius
Flexible provisioning of GPUs, TPUs, and CPUs with automatic failover to available, cheaper infrastructure
Autostop and binpacking to clean up idle resources and pack workloads onto shared clusters
Job queueing, multi-node and gang scheduling, and automatic recovery of failed jobs
Local-style development on Kubernetes: SSH into pods, sync code, or connect your IDE
BYOC design: clusters launch inside your own cloud accounts, VPCs, and Kubernetes clusters

gettingStarted.intro: Install SkyPilot with the cloud and Kubernetes backends you need, then launch your first cluster from a short task YAML.

steps.heading: Install SkyPilot
steps.body: Install with uv (pip, nightly, and from-source are also supported) and pick the infra extras you plan to use. This example enables Kubernetes and the major clouds.

steps.heading: Define a task
steps.body: Write a task YAML that declares the resources you want, a setup step, and the command to run. If you omit infra, SkyPilot picks the cheapest option for you.
steps.code:
resources:
  infra: k8s/coreweave
  accelerators: B200:8

workdir: .

setup: |
  uv pip install torch

run: |
  echo "Hello, SkyPilot!"
  nvidia-smi

steps.heading: Launch and check status
steps.body: Provision a cluster and run the task with sky launch, then view your clusters with sky status.
steps.code:
sky launch -c mycluster hello_sky.yaml
sky status

useCases: Running model training or fine-tuning jobs on whichever cloud has the cheapest available GPUs
Giving an infra team one control plane over multiple Kubernetes and Slurm clusters
Queueing and auto-recovering many batch jobs across a shared GPU fleet
Spinning up an interactive dev environment on Kubernetes with code sync and IDE access
